title: botera — Infrastructure & Operations Curriculum
description: A practical, hands-on path from absolute beginner to expert across Linux, Windows, networking, security, cloud, DevOps, SRE and platform engineering. Every lesson includes explanations, examples, hands-on labs, exercises, troubleshooting drills, an assessment with passing requirements, and validated solutions.

tracks.name: Linux Fundamentals
tracks.summary: Absolute basics to confident power-user: the shell, filesystem, files, text, permissions, processes and the help system.

tracks.name: Linux System Administration
tracks.summary: Run real servers: users & groups at scale, systemd, packages, storage & LVM, networking config, logging, scheduling, backups and tuning.

tracks.name: Shell & Bash Scripting
tracks.summary: From your first script to robust, production-grade automation: variables, control flow, functions, text processing, traps, and safe Bash.

tracks.name: Python for Automation
tracks.summary: Python for operators: files, subprocess, APIs, parsing, virtualenvs, packaging and building reliable ops tooling.

tracks.name: Networking
tracks.summary: OSI & TCP/IP, addressing & subnetting, routing & switching, DNS, DHCP, NAT, VLANs, firewalls and methodical network troubleshooting.

tracks.name: Windows Administration
tracks.summary: Windows client and server fundamentals: the OS model, the registry, services, users, NTFS permissions, the event log and management tooling.

tracks.name: Windows Server & Active Directory
tracks.summary: Domains, Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS/DHCP on Windows, file & print services, certificate services and AD hardening.

tracks.name: PowerShell
tracks.summary: Object-oriented automation for Windows and beyond: the pipeline, cmdlets, scripting, remoting, modules and DSC.

tracks.name: Security & Defensive Operations
tracks.summary: Threat models, hardening, identity, cryptography basics, firewalls/IDS, logging & SIEM, vulnerability management and incident response.

tracks.name: Git & Version Control
tracks.summary: Version control mastery: commits, branching, merging, rebasing, remotes, pull requests and team workflows that underpin CI/CD.

tracks.name: Docker & Containers
tracks.summary: Containers from first principles: images, Dockerfiles, volumes, networking, Compose, registries and production image hygiene.

tracks.name: Kubernetes
tracks.summary: Orchestration at scale: pods, deployments, services, config, storage, RBAC, Helm, operators and cluster troubleshooting.

tracks.name: CI/CD Pipelines
tracks.summary: Continuous integration & delivery: pipelines, build/test/deploy stages, artifacts, environments and progressive delivery.

tracks.name: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
tracks.summary: Declarative infrastructure: providers, resources, state, modules, workspaces and safe plan/apply workflows.

tracks.name: Configuration Management (Ansible)
tracks.summary: Agentless automation: inventories, playbooks, roles, variables, templates, idempotence and fleet management.

tracks.name: Cloud Fundamentals
tracks.summary: Cloud concepts that transfer everywhere: compute, storage, networking, identity, regions, billing and the shared-responsibility model — with AWS, Azure and GCP examples.

tracks.name: Monitoring, Logging & Observability
tracks.summary: See your systems: metrics with Prometheus, dashboards with Grafana, logs with the ELK/Loki stack, tracing, alerting and SLOs.

tracks.name: Databases & Data Infrastructure
tracks.summary: Operate data stores: SQL foundations, PostgreSQL/MySQL administration, backups & PITR, replication, indexing and performance.

tracks.name: Web Infrastructure
tracks.summary: Serve the web reliably: Nginx & HAProxy, reverse proxies, TLS/Let's Encrypt, caching, load balancing and CDNs.

tracks.name: Virtualization
tracks.summary: Hypervisors and VMs: KVM/QEMU, libvirt, VMware, Hyper-V and Proxmox, plus snapshots, templates and resource control.

tracks.name: Storage & Filesystems
tracks.summary: Disks to distributed storage: partitions, filesystems, LVM, RAID, NFS/SMB, iSCSI and object storage.

tracks.name: Backup, DR & High Availability
tracks.summary: Survive failure: backup strategy (3-2-1), restore testing, RPO/RTO, clustering, failover and disaster-recovery planning.

tracks.name: SRE & Platform Engineering
tracks.summary: Reliability as engineering: SLI/SLO/error budgets, toil reduction, capacity, on-call, platforms, golden paths and incident command.

tracks.name: Troubleshooting & Incident Response
tracks.summary: A repeatable method for diagnosing anything: systematic troubleshooting, performance analysis (USE/RED), debugging and blameless postmortems.
